title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a local historical exhibition.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I went to the town hall yesterday to see the 'Victorian Origins' display. It’s quite a comprehensive collection they’ve put together. || Woman: I enjoyed it too. [1]What I found most compelling was the section on the development of the local railway system[/1]. It really showed how the town transformed overnight. || Man: It was informative, but [2]I found the lack of interactive elements for younger visitors a bit of a missed opportunity[/2]. It was mostly just long walls of text. || Woman: True, it felt a bit old-fashioned in its presentation. My kids were bored within ten minutes, despite the interesting subject matter. || Man: Exactly. A few digital screens would have gone a long way in making it more engaging for a modern audience.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I went to the town hall yesterday to see the 'Victorian Origins' display. It’s quite a comprehensive collection they’ve put together.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I enjoyed it too. What I found most compelling was the section on the development of the local railway system. It really showed how the town transformed overnight.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It was informative, but I found the lack of interactive elements for younger visitors a bit of a missed opportunity. It was mostly just long walls of text.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: True, it felt a bit old-fashioned in its presentation. My kids were bored within ten minutes, despite the interesting subject matter.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly. A few digital screens would have gone a long way in making it more engaging for a modern audience.

questions.question: What part of the exhibition did the woman find most interesting?

options.B: the impact of transport on the town
options.C: the personal stories of factory workers

questions.explanation: She states that the development of the railway system was 'most compelling' [1].

questions.question: The man suggests that the exhibition

options.B: was too small for the number of visitors.
options.C: failed to cater for a diverse age group.

questions.explanation: He mentions the lack of interactive elements for 'younger visitors' as a missed opportunity [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new office recycling policy.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you seen the new multi-coloured bins in the canteen? Management is getting serious about our zero-waste targets. || Woman: It’s a good initiative. But [3]I suspect the main motivation is to cut down on the rising costs of commercial waste disposal[/3]. || Man: Perhaps, but the environmental benefit is still there. I’m just finding the new sorting system a bit confusing. || Woman: [4]I’ve already noticed that people are putting food waste in the paper bin[/4], which ruins the whole batch for recycling anyway. It’s a bit frustrating. || Man: I agree. We need much clearer signage on the bins themselves. || Woman: Or maybe a quick training session. Without proper education, it’s just a box-ticking exercise for the company.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you seen the new multi-coloured bins in the canteen? Management is getting serious about our zero-waste targets.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s a good initiative. But I suspect the main motivation is to cut down on the rising costs of commercial waste disposal.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Perhaps, but the environmental benefit is still there. I’m just finding the new sorting system a bit confusing.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve already noticed that people are putting food waste in the paper bin, which ruins the whole batch for recycling anyway. It’s a bit frustrating.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. We need much clearer signage on the bins themselves.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Or maybe a quick training session. Without proper education, it’s just a box-ticking exercise for the company.

questions.question: The woman believes the new policy was introduced primarily to
questions.explanation: She suspects the motive is to 'cut down on the rising costs' of waste disposal [3].

questions.question: What problem does the woman identify with the current situation?

options.A: the bins being emptied too infrequently
options.B: the incorrect use of the recycling facilities
options.C: the lack of space for the new bins

questions.explanation: She notes that people are putting 'food waste in the paper bin' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new mobile phone they have both bought.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, how are you getting on with the new 'Titan' smartphone? I’m still blown away by the camera quality. || Woman: The photos are stunning. But [5]I’ve found the facial recognition feature to be quite temperamental[/5], especially in low light. It takes me three tries just to unlock my phone. || Man: Really? I haven't had that issue. I was more concerned about the battery life, but it seems to hold up quite well. || Woman: [6]I just wish the manufacturers hadn't removed the headphone jack[/6]. I’m fed up with having to remember a separate adapter every time I want to use my good earphones. || Man: I agree, it’s a bit of an inconvenience. They’re basically forcing us all to go wireless whether we like it or not.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, how are you getting on with the new 'Titan' smartphone? I’m still blown away by the camera quality.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The photos are stunning. But I’ve found the facial recognition feature to be quite temperamental, especially in low light. It takes me three tries just to unlock my phone.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Really? I haven't had that issue. I was more concerned about the battery life, but it seems to hold up quite well.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I just wish the manufacturers hadn't removed the headphone jack. I’m fed up with having to remember a separate adapter every time I want to use my good earphones.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it’s a bit of an inconvenience. They’re basically forcing us all to go wireless whether we like it or not.

questions.question: The woman expresses disappointment with the phone's
questions.explanation: She describes the facial recognition (biometric) feature as 'temperamental' [5].

questions.question: What design choice do both speakers disagree with?

options.A: the size of the screen display
options.B: the lack of a physical port for audio
options.C: the placement of the charging socket

questions.explanation: They both complain about the removal of the 'headphone jack' [6].
